mcqs.question: Which of the following statements best describes Newton's First Law of Motion?
What happens to a stationary object when no external force acts on it?
Which of the following is an example of Newton's First Law of Motion?
In which situation does an object experience inertia?

fill_in_the_blanks.question: An object at rest will remain at rest unless acted upon by an __________ force.
The property of an object to resist changes in its state of motion is called __________.
According to Newton's First Law, an object in motion will continue in its state of motion unless acted upon by an __________ force.
The First Law of Motion is also known as the Law of __________.
The tendency of a moving object to keep moving in a straight line is known as __________.

short_questions.question: What is Newton's First Law of Motion?
short_questions.answer: It states that an object at rest will remain at rest, and an object in motion will continue in motion unless acted upon by an external force.

short_questions.question: Define inertia.
short_questions.answer: Inertia is the tendency of an object to resist changes in its state of motion.

short_questions.question: Give an example of Newton's First Law in everyday life.
short_questions.answer: When a car suddenly stops, passengers lurch forward due to inertia.

short_questions.question: How does Newton's First Law apply to a hockey puck sliding on ice?
short_questions.answer: The puck continues to slide on the ice until friction or another force stops it.

short_questions.question: What role does friction play in Newton's First Law?
short_questions.answer: Friction is an external force that can stop or slow down moving objects.
